title: Your Best-Ranked Page Might Be Invisible to Google's AI
tldr: Top organic Google rankings no longer guarantee citation in AI Overviews, breaking the old SEO playbook. A signal that content discoverability now depends on generative engine optimization.
angle: Write a primer on GEO (generative engine optimization) and why content teams must rethink what 'ranking' means.

title: The #1 Role Your Content Team Needs in 2026 Is a Managing Editor
tldr: As AI makes content cheap to produce, editorial judgment becomes the differentiator, making the managing editor the key hire. A clear argument for human curation in an automated workflow.
why_fresh: reframes team structure for AI era
angle: Argue that editorial judgment is the scarce resource in the AI content flood and what a modern managing editor actually does.

title: Why "AI Productivity Gains" Is the Wrong Pitch for Every Stakeholder Above You
tldr: Pitching AI as a productivity win fails to move CMOs, CFOs and legal; each needs the metric reframed to defend headcount and unlock budget. Practical framing advice for comms leaders.
angle: Build a translation table for pitching AI initiatives to each C-suite stakeholder in their own language.

title: 'Social media is now three distinct categories'
tldr: Researchers argue platforms have split into broadcasters, micro-communities, and AI-driven active experiences. A useful new taxonomy for social strategy.
angle: Map your own channel strategy onto the broadcaster / micro-community / AI triad and what each demands.

title: The Future of Content Belongs to the Tastemakers
tldr: With AI making polished copy trivial, distinctive taste and point of view become the differentiator that makes content memorable. A craft-over-volume manifesto.
angle: Explore how 'taste' becomes a defensible content asset when production is commoditized by AI.

title: Why communicators are trading employee engagement for employee experience
tldr: Internal comms is moving from vanity metrics (clicks, opens) toward measurable business outcomes, especially for frontline workers. A maturity shift in internal communications measurement.
angle: Examine how to tie internal communication to business outcomes rather than engagement vanity metrics.

title: Optimizing press releases for GEO and journalists: See a real example
tldr: MD Anderson restructured a press release to serve both AI search engines and human journalists, with a worked example. Concrete tactics for the GEO era of media relations.
angle: Show how to write press releases that earn AI citations without losing human-journalist appeal.

title: The Rundown: AI clones split the creator economy
tldr: Unauthorized AI voice clones versus authorized digital twins are dividing creators, brands and lawyers over consent and value. A live IP and ethics fault line for synthetic media.
angle: Unpack the consent economics of digital twins versus voice clones for brands working with creators.

title: What kind of stories turn local news readers into subscribers? Hard news, not the soft stuff
tldr: New data shows hard news (government, public health, politics) converts subscribers far better than soft content. Counterintuitive evidence for editorial planning.
angle: Use the subscriber data to argue that substance, not snackable content, drives loyalty and conversion.

title: The Real Reason Organizations Struggle to Build Brand Trust
tldr: Brand trust comes from emotionally connected, integrated marketing, not endless content volume. A reframe of trust as a systems and emotion problem.
angle: Connect brand trust to integration across channels rather than content quantity.

title: Time's editor-in-chief on AI, relationships and what makes a good pitch
tldr: Time's Sam Jacobs says AI is reshaping distribution but human relationships remain central to PR and journalism. Insider view on media outreach in the AI era.
angle: Distill what makes pitches land when AI handles discovery but editors crave human connection.

title: How to Write Content That Lands With Decision Makers
tldr: Output is up but senior buyers are unimpressed; a practical guide to B2B thought leadership that moves contract-signers. Bridges the gap between dashboard health and real influence.
angle: Contrast vanity content metrics with what actually persuades B2B decision-makers.

title: Podcasts and influencers least culturally relevant media
tldr: Thinkbox study finds TV still holds the largest share of cultural influence while podcasts and creators rank lowest. Provocative data that cuts against creator-economy hype.
angle: Question whether 'cultural relevance' is the right metric, or whether the study undersells niche influence.

title: Five Trends Redefining Brand Storytelling
tldr: Edelman outlines five shifts reshaping how brands tell stories. A trend-spotting piece useful for benchmarking narrative strategy.
angle: React to Edelman's trends and add a contrarian take on which actually matter for comms teams.

title: The Anti-AI Brand Is Becoming a Real Market Position
tldr: Rejecting AI is emerging as a genuine brand differentiator and positioning play. A fresh angle on how AI backlash creates marketing opportunity.
angle: Explore 'human-made' as a deliberate brand stance and when anti-AI positioning is credible vs. gimmicky.
